user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
so this is OpEx today, 
we have AM and PM expirations as always.
this si bcs the SPX options are european style contracts, so they can be only exercised at expo days.
The AM contracts stopped trading with the thursday close, and the SPXW contracts will trade until today's PM.
The value will be determined this morning at 9.30, thats when the auction starts to calculate the opening index level, determined by aggregating quotations, orders submitted at or just before market open.
This lvl becomes the exercise‐settlement value. This value will be the benchmark to use to settle any ITM options.
For calls it is (Settlement Value – Strike Price)
For puts it is (Strike Price – Settlement Value)
multiplied by 100 (the contract multiplier) 
Then everybody gets his money or not, depending on if his contract expires ITM or not
comment.raw_body:

The value will be determined this morning at 9.30, thats when the auction starts to calculate the opening index level, determined by aggregating quotations, orders submitted at or just before market open.
This lvl becomes the exercise‐settlement value. This value will be the benchmark to use to settle any ITM options.
For calls it is (Settlement Value – Strike Price)
For puts it is (Strike Price – Settlement Value)
multiplied by 100 (the contract multiplier) 
Then everybody gets his money or not, depending on if his contract expires ITM or not

comment.body:
we all know how a fraction works: 
if numerator = denominator, then the ratio is 1
if numerator > denominator, then the ratio is greater than 1
if numerator < denominator, the ratio is lower than 1

comment.body:
these values tells us that:
p/c shows that MMs are shorter puts than calls, bcs the ratio is 6.18x bigger than 1

the p/c delta ratios however telling us that there is a bit more call deltas in their inventories... so MMs short ITM calls (same side of the smirk btw as short OTM puts, so vanna/charm effect remains)

and finally the p/c ratio again tells us more gamma from puts than from calls.

following from the ratios solely, we can tell what we can also see on the chart that MMs are short skew this AM, and so they have long vanna exposure and short charm , and this is what held the market up yesterday, gave positive response to vol increase, and holds the market up during overnight.
(not every firms are hedging during overnight sessions, and not with that magnitude as tehy do RTH, but its there)
